prediction.summary: An internal host (10.20.3.15) associated with the svc_etl user initiated a large data transfer of approximately 1.1 GB to an external, potentially malicious domain, upload.evil-cdn.net (185.220.101.44). Data Loss Prevention (DLP) detected FINANCE_PII within the outbound transfer, which was identified as /data/finance_q2.zip uploaded via curl. This activity triggered multiple alerts, including a critical SIEM alert for data exfiltration and an IDS alert for MITRE T1041 (Exfiltration Over C2 Channel).

timeline.event: User svc_etl from 10.20.3.15 connects to upload.evil-cdn.net:443.
Firewall detects outbound anomaly: 10.20.3.15 sent 1.1GB to 185.220.101.44 in 77 seconds.
DLP detects sensitive FINANCE_PII label in outbound transfer by svc_etl.
SIEM identifies data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to rare external domain.
IDS suspects MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel from host 10.20.3.15.
Proxy logs TLS SNI upload.evil-cdn.net from client svc_etl.
Netflow detects volume spike (14x baseline) from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
SIEM issues CRITICAL alert for data exfiltration by svc_etl to upload.evil-cdn.net.
EDR reports curl process initiated by svc_etl uploading /data/finance_q2.zip.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 from the network to prevent further data loss or command and control.
Investigate the svc_etl user account for compromise, including password resets, MFA review, and recent activity.
Analyze the /data/finance_q2.zip file and its origin on the compromised host to understand the scope of data exposure.
Perform forensic analysis on host 10.20.3.15 to identify initial compromise vector, persistence mechanisms, and other affected systems.
Block upload.evil-cdn.net and 185.220.101.44 at the perimeter firewall and proxy.
Notify legal and privacy teams regarding the potential exfiltration of FINANCE_PII.
